motion: Merge PR #32 (epic: End-to-End QA) — verify the Playwright E2E suite genuinely validates SeeKi against the real MEC-Miki PostgreSQL database (vehicle_logs and SOC tables)

QUESTIONER.note: No rounds filed; all cited claims evaluated on source evidence directly

findings.description: seed.sql is never applied by global-setup.ts — CI reproducibility gap. Session 2 'ALL PASSING' reflects a manually-prepared DB state that is not reproducible on fresh checkout. On a clean DB, NULL/boolean/numeric formatting tests and two-table navigation would skip silently.
findings.fix_description: Add a seed.sql execution step after waitForHealthy() using psql with the same env vars used for seeki.toml.test, or invoke via the Rust binary's existing DB pool. See recommendation file for sample implementation.

findings.description: Filter narrowing assertion has no lower bound — zero-match filter result passes silently. `expect(filteredTotal).toBeLessThanOrEqual(initialTotal)` is satisfied by 0, so a structurally broken filter that always returns zero rows passes the test.
findings.fix_description: Add `expect(filteredTotal).toBeGreaterThan(0)` before the existing upper-bound assertion at L131 to confirm the filter actually matched rows.

findings.description: DB identity is unverified — a reachable wrong DB (developer local convenience DB) produces a green suite identical to MEC-Miki. This is a consequence of the suite being generic by design; not a test logic bug.
findings.fix_description: PR description amendment only (recharacterize suite as generic behavioral harness). Optional: add assert that `/api/tables` includes expected table names when SEEKI_E2E_ASSERT_MEC_MIKI=1 is set.

critical_discoveries.description: Config backup at global-setup.ts:98-103 is corrupted on interrupted retry — Run #1 killed after line 103 leaves test config in seeki.toml; Run #2 then copies that test config over seeki.toml.user-backup, permanently destroying the user's original config.
critical_discoveries.recommended_fix: Before overwriting CONFIG_BACKUP, only write if backup does not exist OR existing backup is identical to CONFIG_DST (indicating prior test-config state). See recommendation file for sample.

conditions: Condition 1: Apply seed.sql by the harness (global-setup.ts) to make the test state reproducible
Condition 2: Add `expect(filteredTotal).toBeGreaterThan(0)` lower bound to the filter test at data-grid.spec.ts:131

PR description amendment: recharacterize suite as a generic behavioral harness that exercises MEC-Miki runtime characteristics (booleans, NULLs, two tables, sortable id) — not a schema-specific regression detector

advocate_conceded: seed.sql is not auto-applied — CI reproducibility gap (F1)
DB identity unverified — reachable wrong DB produces green suite (F3 / Gap 4)
Config backup corrupted on interrupted retry (CD-1 / Gap 3)
Filter assertion has no lower bound (F2 / C4)
NULL/boolean formatting tests are soft coverage contingent on live page-1 data (C3)
Suite cannot detect MEC-Miki SCHEMA-specific regressions (column renames, type changes, timestamp format)
critics_conceded: Empty DB (0 rows / 0 tables) produces hard failures — not silent green
Unreachable host / bad credentials causes setup abort (30s timeout)
Sort conditional IS entered for MEC-Miki's id SERIAL column (C5 for MEC-Miki case)
Filter practical impact: returns positive matches on real MEC-Miki data (C4 practical narrowing)

prior_session_status.session_1_findings: ALL FIXED (6 items in commits dd940dd, 070c245, 2023373)
prior_session_status.session_3_vs_prior: No prior findings re-raised — all prior fixes confirmed intact

summary: Session 3 narrowly scoped to MEC-Miki validation rigor. Council converged on: suite IS a genuine behavioral harness against real MEC-Miki (not a mock-through), but 3 gaps undermine the 'MEC-Miki-specific regression coverage' framing: (F1) seed.sql not auto-applied, (F2) filter missing `>0` lower bound, (F3) DB identity unverified. Plus CD-1 data-loss path in config backup on interrupted retry. Verdict CONDITIONAL — 2 merge blockers + PR description amendment + 1 informational CD.
